member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.KafkaBuilderExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for adding Kafka resources to the application model.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.KafkaBuilderExtensions.AddKafka(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Adds a Kafka resource to the application. A container is used for local development.
member.remarks: This version of the package defaults to the tag of the container image.
member.param: The name of the resource. This name will be used as the connection string name when referenced in a dependency
The host port of Kafka broker.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.KafkaBuilderExtensions.WithKafkaUI(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.KafkaServerResource},System.Action{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.KafkaUIContainerResource}},System.String)
member.summary: Adds a Kafka UI container to the application.
member.remarks: This version of the package defaults to the tag of the container image.
member.param: Configuration callback for KafkaUI container resource.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.KafkaBuilderExtensions.WithHostPort(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.KafkaUIContainerResource},System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Configures the host port that the KafkaUI resource is exposed on instead of using randomly assigned port.
member.param: The port to bind on the host. If is used random port will be assigned.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.KafkaBuilderExtensions.WithDataVolume(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.KafkaServerResource},System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds a named volume for the data folder to a Kafka container resource.
member.param: The name of the volume. Defaults to an auto-generated name based on the application and resource names.
A flag that indicates if this is a read-only volume.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.KafkaBuilderExtensions.WithDataBindMount(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.KafkaServerResource},System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds a bind mount for the data folder to a Kafka container resource.
member.param: The source directory on the host to mount into the container.
A flag that indicates if this is a read-only mount.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.KafkaBuilderExtensions.ConfigureLogDirs(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EnvironmentCallbackContext)
member.summary: Only need to call this if we want to persistent kafka data

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.KafkaServerResource
member.summary: A resource that represents a Kafka broker.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.KafkaServerResource.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: A resource that represents a Kafka broker.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.KafkaServerResource.PrimaryEndpoint
member.summary: Gets the primary endpoint for the Kafka broker. This endpoint is used for host processes to Kafka broker communication. To connect to the Kafka broker from a container, use .

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.KafkaServerResource.Host
member.summary: Gets the host endpoint reference for the primary endpoint.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.KafkaServerResource.Port
member.summary: Gets the port endpoint reference for the primary endpoint.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.KafkaServerResource.InternalEndpoint
member.summary: Gets the internal endpoint for the Kafka broker. This endpoint is used for container to broker communication. To connect to the Kafka broker from a host process, use .

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.KafkaServerResource.ConnectionStringExpression
member.summary: Gets the connection string expression for the Kafka broker.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.KafkaUIContainerResource
member.summary: A resource that represents a Kafka UI container.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.KafkaUIContainerResource.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: A resource that represents a Kafka UI container.
